text_en: Recognizing that desertification and drought are global problems in that they affect all regions of the world and that concerted efforts by the international community are needed to combat desertification or mitigate the effects of drought, including through the integration of poverty eradication strategies,

items_en: 1. Takes note of the report of the Secretary-General; 2

2. Welcomes the outcome of the fourth session of the Conference of the Parties to the United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification in Those Countries Experiencing Serious Drought and/or Desertification, Particularly in Africa, including the adoption of the Declaration on Commitments to Strengthen the Implementation of the Obligations of the Convention 3;
3. Also welcomes the outcome of the fifth session of the Conference of the Parties;

4. Notes the establishment of the Committee for the Review of the Implementation of the Convention as a subsidiary body of the Conference of the Parties, and invites Parties and other interested partners to participate in the first session of the Committee, to be held in Bonn, Germany, from 18 to 29 November 2002, in accordance with decision 2/COP.5 of the Conference of the Parties of 12 October 2001; 4

5. Also notes that the Committee may be renewed in its mandate and functions by the Conference of the Parties at its seventh session, taking into account the lessons learned from the overall evaluation of the Committee 5;

6. Calls upon the conferences of the parties to the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change 6 , the Convention on Biological Diversity 7 , the Convention to Combat Desertification and other international instruments relating to the environment and sustainable development, and their respective secretariats, as well as other relevant organizations, in particular the United Nations Environment Programme, including, as appropriate, with the participation of the Environmental Management Group, to continue their work aimed at enhancing their complementarity, with full respect for the status of the secretariats of the conventions and the autonomous decision-making prerogatives of the conferences of the parties to the conventions in question, to enhance their cooperation in promoting progress in the implementation of those conventions at the international, regional and national levels, and to report thereon to the conferences of the parties concerned;

7. Welcomes the decision of the Conference of the Parties to the Convention to Combat Desertification to present, as a contribution to the preparatory process for the World Summit on Sustainable Development, the President's summary of the special meeting and the high-level interactive discussion of the fifth session of the Conference of the Parties, including issues related to combating desertification, controlling land degradation and mitigating the effects of drought in affected developing countries, as well as opportunities in these areas, including aspects related to financial resources and sustainable development, 8 and the comprehensive report of the United Nations Development Group on the Convention to Combat Desertification,

Demande Ã   la  communautÃĐ  internationale  de  contribuer  Ã   la  mise  en Åuvre de ces programmes par le biais, notamment, d'accords de partenariats et des programmes de coopÃĐration bilatÃĐrale et multilatÃĐrale offerts pour l'application de la Convention, y compris de contributions d'organisations non gouvernementales et du secteur privÃĐ, pour appuyer les efforts faits par les pays en dÃĐveloppement pour appliquer la Convention ;

13. Note avec satisfaction les mesures que prennent les pays en dÃĐveloppement touchÃĐs qui sont parties Ã  la Convention, avec l'aide d'organisations internationales et de leurs partenaires bilatÃĐraux de dÃĐveloppement, pour appliquer la Convention, et les efforts qui sont faits pour promouvoir la participation de tous les  acteurs  de  la  sociÃĐtÃĐ  civile  Ã   l'ÃĐlaboration  et  Ã   l'exÃĐcution  de  programmes d'action nationaux de lutte contre la dÃĐsertification et, Ã  ce propos, engage les pays Ã  coopÃĐrer aux ÃĐchelons sous-rÃĐgional et rÃĐgional, selon qu'il convient ;

items_en: 8. Also welcomes the decision of the Council of the Global Environment Facility, under agenda item 7 of its December 2001 meeting, to consider at its next meeting the proposed amendments to the Instrument for the Restructuring of the Global Environment Facility 10 with a view to designating land degradation, primarily desertification and deforestation, as a major focus of the Fund in order to increase its support for the effective implementation of the Convention, with a view to recommending to the Assembly of the Global Environment Facility that they be approved at its meeting in October 2002;

9. Encourages the Conference of the Parties and the Council and the Assembly of the Global Environment Facility to work together and effectively to facilitate the financing of the full implementation of the Convention by the Fund, in order to achieve the objectives set by the Convention to halt land degradation, primarily desertification and deforestation;

10. Notes with appreciation that some affected developing countries have adopted programmes of action at the national, subregional and regional levels, and urges those countries that have not yet done so to accelerate the preparation and adoption of their programmes of action with a view to finalizing them as soon as possible;

11. Calls upon the international community to contribute to the implementation of those programmes through, inter alia, partnership agreements and bilateral and multilateral cooperation programmes for the implementation of the Convention, including contributions from non-governmental organizations and the private sector, in support of the efforts of developing countries to implement the Convention;

12. Invites affected developing countries to make the implementation of their programmes of action to combat desertification one of the main priority themes of their dialogue with their development partners;

13. Notes with appreciation the measures being taken by affected developing countries that are parties to the Convention, with the assistance of international organizations and their bilateral development partners, to implement the Convention, and the efforts being made to promote the participation of all civil society actors in the preparation and implementation of national action programmes to combat desertification, and in this regard encourages countries to cooperate at the subregional and regional levels, as appropriate;

14. Welcomes the enhanced cooperation between the Convention secretariat and the Global Mechanism, and encourages further efforts in this regard to ensure the effective implementation of the Convention;

15. Invites all parties to pay their contributions to the core budget of the Convention for the biennium 2002-2003 without delay, and urges all parties that have not yet done so to pay their contributions for 1999 and/or the biennium 2000-2001 as soon as possible, in order to ensure that cash flows continue to ensure the continuity of the activities of the Conference of the Parties, the secretariat and the Global Mechanism;

items_en: 16. Calls upon Governments, multilateral financial institutions, regional development banks, regional economic integration organizations and all other interested organizations, as well as non-governmental organizations and the private sector, to make generous contributions to the General Fund, the Supplementary Fund and the Special Fund, in accordance with the relevant paragraphs of the financial rules of the Conference of the Parties, and welcomes the financial support already provided by some countries;

17. Decides to include in the calendar of conferences and meetings for the biennium 2002-2003 the sessions of the Conference of the Parties and its subsidiary bodies, including the sixth regular session of the Conference of the Parties and the meetings of its subsidiary bodies;

18. Requests the United Nations Development Programme to continue to implement decision 2000/23 of the Governing Council of the United Nations Development Programme of 29 September 2000 on cooperation between the United Nations Development Programme and the secretariat of the Convention in order to refocus activities to combat desertification at the national, subregional and regional levels; 12

19. Approves the continuation of existing institutional links and related administrative arrangements between the United Nations Secretariat and the Convention secretariat for a further period of five years, to be reviewed by the General Assembly and the Conference of the Parties by 31 December 2006;
20. Requests the Secretary-General to report to the General Assembly at its fifty-seventh session on the implementation of the present resolution;

21. Decides to include in the provisional agenda of its fifty-seventh session the sub-item entitled "Implementation of the United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification in Those Countries Experiencing Serious Drought and/or Desertification, Particularly in Africa".
